parameterGroups.label: CC Mapping
parameterGroups.rationale: Both parameters define the pair of CC numbers to swap - they are symmetric and only meaningful together

parameters.name: First CC
parameters.description: First controller number that gets swapped

parameters.name: Second CC
parameters.description: Second controller number that gets swapped

gaps.question: How does processHiseEvent() implement the swap? Does it check incoming CC number against both FirstCC and SecondCC and replace the matching one with the other, or is there a different mechanism?
gaps.context: Need to describe the exact transformation for the reference page pseudo-code.

gaps.question: Are non-CC MIDI events (noteOn, noteOff, pitch bend, aftertouch) passed through unchanged?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether this module affects any MIDI data beyond CC messages.

gaps.question: What happens when FirstCC and SecondCC are set to the same value? Is it a no-op, or does it cause unexpected behavior? What about the default state where both are 0?
gaps.context: Both parameters default to 0 - users need to know if this edge case is handled gracefully.

gaps.question: Does the swap preserve the CC value (velocity/amount) unchanged, or is the value modified in any way during the swap?
gaps.context: Users remapping controllers need to know that only the CC number changes, not the value.

notes: CCSwapper is one of the simplest MIDI processors - a pure CC number remapper with no modulation, no audio, and no interfaces. Both parameters default to 0, which means CC #0 (Bank Select MSB) would be swapped with itself at default settings - effectively a no-op until configured. The symmetric nature of the swap means order of FirstCC/SecondCC does not matter.
